reasoning_chain: The arid, dry vegetation and sparse tree cover are typical of northern Mexico’s semi-desert regions.
The unpaved dirt road with no markings or signage suggests a rural area common in less developed parts of Mexico.
Distant mountains and flat agricultural fields align with the topography of states like Sonora or Chihuahua.
